title: Quality assurance technicians
description: Apply theory and principles of environmental engineering to modify, test, and operate equipment and devices used in the prevention, control, and remediation of environmental problems, including waste treatment and site remediation, under the direction of engineering staff or scientists. May assist in the development of environmental remediation devices. Apply engineering theory and principles to problems of industrial layout or manufacturing production, usually under the direction of engineering staff. May perform time and motion studies on worker operations in a variety of industries for purposes such as establishing standard production rates or improving efficiency. Implement production processes and operate commercial-scale production equipment to produce, test, or modify materials, devices, or systems of unique molecular or macromolecular composition. Operate advanced microscopy equipment to manipulate nanoscale objects. Work under the supervision of nanoengineering staff. (Based on 25 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Conduct environmental audits, inspections, or investigations of violations, and monitor the environmental impact of development activities, pollution, or land degradation.

Forecast future status or condition of ecosystems based on changing industrial practices or environmental conditions, and investigate the impact of changed land management or land use practices on ecosystems.

Build and maintain databases of information about energy alternatives, pollutants, natural environments, industrial processes, and other information related to ecological change, and gather information from geographic information systems (GIS) databases or applications to formulate land use recommendations.

Carry out environmental assessments in accordance with applicable standards, regulations, or laws, and conduct site assessments to certify a habitat or ascertain environmental damage or restoration needs.
Conduct scientific protection, mitigation, or restoration projects to prevent resource damage, maintain the integrity of critical habitats, and minimize the impact of human activities.
Create complex and dynamic mathematical models of population, community, or ecological systems, and develop or test protocols to monitor ecosystem components and ecological processes.

Plan or conduct field research on topics such as industrial production, industrial ecology, population ecology, and environmental production or sustainability, and develop environmental studies, such as plant material field trials or wildlife habitat impact studies.
Translate the theories of industrial ecology into eco-industrial practices and develop strategies to minimize the environmental impact of industrial production processes.

Plan or conduct studies of the ecological implications of historic or projected changes in industrial processes or development, and monitor projects during or after construction to ensure conformity to design specifications.

Provide industrial managers with technical materials on environmental issues, regulatory guidelines, or compliance actions, and communicate scientific or technical information to the public, organizations, or internal audiences via reports and presentations.

Research sources of pollution to determine environmental impact or develop methods of pollution abatement or control, and investigate the adaptability of various animal and plant species to changed environmental conditions.
Review industrial practices, identify potential liabilities and environmental hazards, and evaluate existing policies or legislation to identify environmental impacts.
Collect, synthesize, analyze, manage, and report environmental data, such as pollution emission measurements and atmospheric monitoring measurements.
Communicate findings of environmental studies and proposals for environmental remediation to other restoration professionals and develop environmental management or restoration plans for sites.

Conduct environmental impact studies to examine the ecological effects of pollutants, disease, human activities, nature, and climate change, and examine local, regional, or global use and flow of materials or energy in industrial production processes.
Conduct analyses to determine the maximum amount of work that can be accomplished for a given amount of energy in a system and analyze changes designed to improve environmental performance.
Develop environmental management or conservation plans, implement soil or water management techniques, and plan soil management or conservation practices.
Identify environmental impacts caused by products, systems, or projects, and develop alternatives to industrial or waste-management practices.
Conduct feasibility and cost-benefit studies for environmental remediation projects and analyze results of investigations to determine measures needed to maintain or restore proper soil management.
Prepare plans to manage renewable resources and compute design specifications for the implementation of conservation practices.

Implement production processes and operate commercial-scale production equipment to produce, test, or modify materials, devices, or systems, using principles of environmental and industrial engineering.

Supervise, train, or provide technical guidance to staff or teams involved in environmental planning and restoration, review existing environmental remediation designs, and oversee compliance with environmental policies and regulations.

Review existing environmental technical standards, guidelines, policies, and formal regulations to ensure compliance with requirements, and provide technical direction on environmental planning to other professionals.
Promote use of environmental management systems (EMS) to reduce waste or improve environmentally sound use of natural resources and develop educational programs on environmental issues.
Research stories regarding the area's natural history or environment.
Construct historical, scientific, and nature visitor-center displays. Prepare brochures and write newspaper articles.
Perform routine maintenance on park structures and assist with operations of general facilities, such as visitor centers.
Plan, organize, and direct activities of seasonal staff members; confer with park staff to determine subjects and schedules for park programs.
Compile and maintain official park photographic and information files. Interview specialists in desired fields to obtain and develop data for park information programs.
Measure and assess vegetation resources for biological assessments, environmental impact statements, and rangeland monitoring programs.

Maintain soil stability and vegetation for non-grazing uses, such as wildlife habitats and outdoor recreation. Perform emergency duties to protect human life, government property, and natural features of the park.
Prepare and present illustrated lectures and interpretive talks about park features.
Evaluate industrial operations for compliance with permits or regulations related to the generation, storage, treatment, transportation, or disposal of hazardous materials or waste.
Prepare and package environmental samples for shipping or testing, and prepare permit applications or review compliance with environmental permits.

Decontaminate or test field equipment used to clean or test pollutants from soil, air, or water. Prepare reports describing contamination tests, material or equipment decontaminated, or methods used in decontamination processes.
Monitor hazardous waste cleanup procedures to ensure proper application of nanocomposites or accomplishment of objectives.
Apply safety tags to equipment needing maintenance and follow policies and procedures for radiation workers to ensure personnel safety.

Inspect or measure plant equipment or field data, including numerical data, test results, photographs, or summaries of visual observations to verify that equipment is being operated and maintained according to quality assurance standards by observing worker performance.

Test selected products at specified stages in the production process for performance characteristics or adherence to specifications, and analyze samples, such as air or water samples, for contaminants or other elements.

Conduct surveillance testing to determine the safety of nuclear equipment and monitor nuclear reactor equipment performance to identify operational inefficiencies, hazards, or needs for maintenance or repair.
Prepare detailed verbal or written presentations for scientists, engineers, project managers, or upper management, and compile and report meteorological data.
Keep records and prepare reports detailing findings, investigative methods, and laboratory techniques.
Collect samples of air, water, gases, or solids to determine radioactivity levels of contamination.

Perform environmental quality work in field or office settings, including maintaining project logbook records and testing or decontaminating equipment. Confer with experts concerning evidence and its interpretation, and analyze gunshot residue and bullet paths to determine how incidents occurred. Collect and store evidence from crime scenes, ensuring integrity, and prepare solutions or reagents needed for laboratory work. Operate and maintain laboratory equipment, conduct routine and non-routine analyses, and perform validations or transfers of analytical methods according to policies or guidelines. Monitor testing procedures to ensure adherence to specifications, identify quality problems, and make recommendations for corrective actions. Participate in internal assessments and audits, compile test data, and write or revise standard operating procedures. Serve as a technical liaison between quality control and other departments. Maintain records, provide quality control data for regulatory submissions, and train other analysts in laboratory procedures.
Recommend equipment modifications or new equipment purchases.
Supervise and participate in the inspection of properties to ensure compliance with applicable regulations and standards.
Inspect and test new and existing systems and equipment to ensure they operate properly.
Study and interpret safety codes to establish procedures for hazardous substance management.
Analyze test data to identify defects or determine calibration needs, calibrate devices according to standards, and document procedures and results of tests.
Disassemble and reassemble equipment for inspection, and maintain or repair measurement devices used for calibration testing.
Conduct calibration tests to determine the performance of mechanical or electromechanical equipment.
Read blueprints or schematics and verify dimensions using precision measuring instruments.
Document and report non-destructive testing processes and results, and interpret results according to applicable standards.
Evaluate material properties and optimize process parameters for photonic or optical systems, including the assembly and adjustment of components.
Assist engineers in the development of new products and fixtures, and test or analyze products according to specified plans.
Produce images or data using radiographic techniques or other imaging methods, and perform inspections for defects in various materials or structures.
Conduct non-destructive testing using techniques such as ultrasonic and thermal testing to identify defects in materials.
Supervise the work of trainees or technicians in non-destructive testing and maintain a clean working environment.

emerging_tasks.task: Apply herbicide to eliminate harmful plants.
Conduct life cycle assessments of products.
Train staff and volunteers on park programs.
Calibrate, maintain, and repair chemical instrumentation sensing elements and sampling system equipment, using calibration instruments and hand tools.
Document results from radiation and contamination surveys.
Inspect, test, and maintain respiratory protection equipment.
Operate drones to capture aerial footage or photographs of crime scenes for further analysis.
Operate drones to monitor and assess fire conditions, track fire progress, and identify safe access points for firefighters.
Operate remote sensing equipment on drones to collect data in areas that are difficult to access or require high-resolution imagery.
Deploy and monitor drones for aerial surveillance and assessment of fire situations.
Operate drones for remote inspection of large or hard-to-reach structures, such as wind turbines, bridges, or tall buildings.
